What changes have been made to the data storage directory in Ditch Assist?Starting from app version 4.20.5, the data storage directory in Ditch Assist has been updated to Android-Data-com.AutoBotz.DitchAssist.MC.
Can the default file manager access the new data storage directory in Ditch Assist?No, the default file manager on your device cannot access the new data storage directory used by Ditch Assist.
Which third-party file manager app is recommended to access the new data storage location?We recommend using Cx File Explorer, a third-party file manager app available on the Play Store, to access the new data storage location in Ditch Assist.
How can Cx File Explorer be used to access the new data storage location in Ditch Assist?To access the new data storage location using Cx File Explorer, install the app, grant the necessary permissions, and navigate to the specified directory where the data is saved.
Are there any additional permissions required when using Cx File Explorer for the first time?When using Cx File Explorer for the first time to access the new data storage location, you may need to grant the app additional permissions to access the specified directory.
What does the Start Elevation on the Grading Screen refer to?The Start Elevation on the Grading Screen refers to the elevation recorded when the 'Start' button is pressed. It is primarily used for tasks where you input a straight grade and then press Start. The target elevation at any given point is calculated based on the straight-line distance from your current location to this starting elevation. However, in grading models generated by Slope-IQ or in imported land leveling designs, this value typically doesn't carry any significance and can usually be disregarded.
What does the Current Elevation on the Grading Screen represent?The Current Elevation on the Grading Screen refers to the elevation at your current position. If you have entered any measurements or calibrations to the GPS height, the value shown here will be the adjusted value based on your inputted offsets.
How is the Distance Covered on the Grading Screen calculated?The Distance Covered on the Grading Screen is the straight-line distance from your current position to the start point. However, this value is not relevant for Slope-IQ or imported land leveling designs.
What does the Current Target Elevation on the Grading Screen represent?The Current Target Elevation on the Grading Screen is the elevation that Ditch Assist aims to reach at your present location. The determination of this value depends on different scenarios. First, in simple grading mode, the target elevation is calculated based on the straight-line distance between the Start Elevation and the Current Elevation. The target elevation is determined according to the grade entered by the user. Second, if a design was created using Slope-IQ and is currently being implemented, the target elevation will be the elevation value on the design that is nearest to your current location. If you're directly over the original survey route used to create the Slope-IQ design, the value will be derived from your location. If your current position is offset from the original survey route, the target value will be taken from the nearest point on the original survey. Finally, if a land leveling design, created in third-party software, has been loaded and selected for implementation, the Current Target Elevation will be based on the computed value from that design.
How does Nudging affect the Current Target Elevation?Nudging adjusts the Current Target Elevation by a certain increment set in the Settings. The Nudge Offset on the Grading Screen tells you how many inches you have 'Nudged' away from the Final Target Elevation. The Final Target Elevation is the same as the Current Target Elevation but is not influenced by Nudge.
What is the purpose of the Target Grade field?The Target Grade field allows you to input the desired gradient for straightforward grading tasks. However, it is not utilized for designs generated by Slope-IQ or imported land leveling designs. Ditch Assist calculates gradients in percentages, so the required grade should be entered as a %. For example, a 5% grade means 5 feet per 100 feet, or 50 feet per 1,000 feet. The Target Grade should be entered as a positive decimal number, such as 0.1, regardless of the direction of travel. The Uphill/Downhill toggle buttons are used to specify whether you are working uphill or downhill.
What is the purpose of the Uphill/Downhill toggle buttons?The Uphill and Downhill toggle buttons on the Grading Screen enable you to determine the starting point (Point A) in relation to the outlet for simple manual grading. This feature is not used when employing Slope-IQ-generated designs or imported land leveling designs. Choosing 'Uphill' tells the app that Point A is the outlet (lowest) point and Point B is uphill from Point A. Consequently, the necessary elevation will INCREASE as you move away from Point A. Selecting 'Downhill' informs the app that Point A is the highest point and Point B is the outlet (located downhill from Point A). Therefore, the necessary elevation will DECREASE as you progress from Point A.
What functions are associated with the Start/Stop/Hold/Reset buttons?Tapping the START button on the Ditching screen enables automatic control (when the valve is installed) and kickstarts the visual up/down guidance. The Target Grade entered serves to calculate the necessary implement height in straightforward grading, whereas the Slope-IQ or imported land leveling design is used to determine the target elevation. Once tapped, the Start button transforms into a STOP button. Pushing STOP immediately switches off automated control and ceases all visual guidance and target elevation computations in the app. Pushing STOP won't remove any loaded parameters, allowing you to use START - STOP - START in succession to continue working on the same design or grading settings. When START is active, the button to its right changes into the HOLD button. HOLD is designed to be used when manual control is necessary, but you still want to make use of the visual aids to observe the current target elevation. Tapping HOLD will pause the energizing of the PWM valve, but all other functions within the app will remain active. The RESET button wipes all current parameters, erasing the current Point A and target grade parameters. After hitting reset, the next time you tap 'Start', a new Point A will be generated from your current location. Long-pressing RESET will clear all map markers and loaded designs from Slope-IQ or imported land leveling designs. It's important to note that if you reset with a land leveling design loaded and wish to reload the same or a different design, you should close and reopen the app to ensure its correct operation.
How is the Start Elevation on the Grading Screen used?The Start Elevation on the Grading Screen is primarily used for tasks where you input a straight grade and then press Start. The target elevation at any given point is calculated based on the straight-line distance from your current location to this starting elevation. However, in grading models generated by Slope-IQ or in imported land leveling designs, this value typically doesn't carry any significance and can usually be disregarded.
What does the Current Elevation on the Grading Screen represent?The Current Elevation on the Grading Screen refers to the elevation at your current position. If you have entered any measurements or calibrations to the GPS height, the value shown here will be the adjusted value based on your inputted offsets.
How is the Distance Covered on the Grading Screen calculated?The Distance Covered on the Grading Screen is the straight-line distance from your current position to the start point. However, this value is not relevant for Slope-IQ or imported land leveling designs.
What does the Current Target Elevation on the Grading Screen represent?The Current Target Elevation on the Grading Screen is the elevation that Ditch Assist aims to reach at your present location. The determination of this value depends on different scenarios. First, in simple grading mode, the target elevation is calculated based on the straight-line distance between the Start Elevation and the Current Elevation. The target elevation is determined according to the grade entered by the user. Second, if a design was created using Slope-IQ and is currently being implemented, the target elevation will be the elevation value on the design that is nearest to your current location. If you're directly over the original survey route used to create the Slope-IQ design, the value will be derived from your location. If your current position is offset from the original survey route, the target value will be taken from the nearest point on the original survey. Finally, if a land leveling design, created in third-party software, has been loaded and selected for implementation, the Current Target Elevation will be based on the computed value from that design.
How does Nudging affect the Current Target Elevation?Nudging adjusts the Current Target Elevation by a certain increment set in the Settings. The Nudge Offset on the Grading Screen tells you how many inches you have 'Nudged' away from the Final Target Elevation. The Final Target Elevation is the same as the Current Target Elevation but is not influenced by Nudge.
What is the purpose of the Target Grade field?The Target Grade field allows you to input the desired gradient for straightforward grading tasks. However, it is not utilized for designs generated by Slope-IQ or imported land leveling designs. Ditch Assist calculates gradients in percentages, so the required grade should be entered as a %. For example, a 5% grade means 5 feet per 100 feet, or 50 feet per 1,000 feet. The Target Grade should be entered as a positive decimal number, such as 0.1, regardless of the direction of travel. The Uphill/Downhill toggle buttons are used to specify whether you are working uphill or downhill.
What is the purpose of the Uphill/Downhill toggle buttons?The Uphill and Downhill toggle buttons on the Grading Screen enable you to determine the starting point (Point A) in relation to the outlet for simple manual grading. This feature is not used when employing Slope-IQ-generated designs or imported land leveling designs. Choosing 'Uphill' tells the app that Point A is the outlet (lowest) point and Point B is uphill from Point A. Consequently, the necessary elevation will INCREASE as you move away from Point A. Selecting 'Downhill' informs the app that Point A is the highest point and Point B is the outlet (located downhill from Point A). Therefore, the necessary elevation will DECREASE as you progress from Point A.
What happens when the START button is tapped on the Ditching screen?Tapping the START button on the Ditching screen enables automatic control (when the valve is installed) and kickstarts the visual up/down guidance. The Target Grade entered serves to calculate the necessary implement height in straightforward grading, whereas the Slope-IQ or imported land leveling design is used to determine the target elevation.
How does the START button change after it is tapped?Once tapped, the Start button transforms into a STOP button.
What happens when the STOP button is pushed?Pushing STOP immediately switches off automated control and ceases all visual guidance and target elevation computations in the app. Pushing STOP won't remove any loaded parameters, allowing you to use START - STOP - START in succession to continue working on the same design or grading settings.
What is the purpose of the HOLD button?When START is active, the button to its right changes into the HOLD button. HOLD is designed to be used when manual control is necessary, but you still want to make use of the visual aids to observe the current target elevation. Tapping HOLD will pause the energizing of the PWM valve, but all other functions within the app will remain active.
What is the function of the RESET button?The RESET button wipes all current parameters, erasing the current Point A and target grade parameters. After hitting reset, the next time you tap 'Start', a new Point A will be generated from your current location.
What happens when the RESET button is long-pressed?Long-pressing RESET will clear all map markers and loaded designs from Slope-IQ or imported land leveling designs.
How should you proceed if you reset with a land leveling design loaded and want to reload the same or a different design?If you reset with a land leveling design loaded and wish to reload the same or a different design, you should close and reopen the app to ensure its correct operation.
What does the Start Elevation on the Grading Screen represent in simple grading mode?In simple grading mode, the Start Elevation on the Grading Screen represents the elevation recorded when the 'Start' button is pressed. It is used to calculate the target elevation based on the straight-line distance between your current location and the starting elevation. The target elevation is determined according to the grade entered by the user. For example, if the elevation at the start point was 100ft and the user input a target grade of 1% working downhill, the current target elevation would be 100 - (100 * 0.01) = 99ft.
How does the Current Target Elevation differ for designs created using Slope-IQ?For designs created using Slope-IQ, the Current Target Elevation on the Grading Screen is based on the elevation value on the design that is nearest to your current location. If you're directly over the original survey route used to create the Slope-IQ design, the value will be derived from your location. If your current position is offset from the original survey route (e.g., to the left or right of it), the target value will be taken from the nearest point on the original survey. This allows you to create a wider drain or grade an entire field based on a single central route survey.
How does the Current Target Elevation differ for imported land leveling designs?For imported land leveling designs, the Current Target Elevation on the Grading Screen is based on the computed value from that design. The elevation is determined according to the specific design and its calculations.
What should be considered when entering the Target Grade?When entering the Target Grade, it should be inputted as a positive decimal number, such as 0.1, regardless of the direction of travel. The Uphill/Downhill toggle buttons are used to specify if you are working uphill or downhill.
What measurements are used for the grades expressed in percentages?The grades expressed in percentages are based on the measurements of 1 foot per 100 feet (or 10 feet per 1,000 feet) or 1 inch per 100 inches (or 10 inches per 1,000 inches). For example, a 5% grade means 5 feet per 100 feet (or 50 feet per 1,000 feet), a 1% grade means 1 foot per 100 feet (or 10 feet per 1,000 feet), a 0.1% grade means 1 foot (12 inches) per 1,000 feet, and a 0.05% grade means 0.5 feet (6 inches) per 1,000 feet.
How should the Uphill/Downhill toggle buttons be used?The Uphill/Downhill toggle buttons should be used to specify the starting point (Point A) in relation to the outlet for simple manual grading. Selecting 'Uphill' indicates that Point A is the outlet (lowest) point and Point B is uphill from Point A. Consequently, the necessary elevation will INCREASE as you move away from Point A. Selecting 'Downhill' indicates that Point A is the highest point and Point B is the outlet (located downhill from Point A). Therefore, the necessary elevation will DECREASE as you progress from Point A. These buttons are not used when employing Slope-IQ-generated designs or imported land leveling designs.
How can the Ditch Assist App be restarted after using the RESET button?After using the RESET button, you can restart the Ditch Assist App by closing and reopening it.
What is the purpose of the Nudge Depth function?The Nudge Depth function is designed to reduce the cut depth when the required depth is too significant to achieve in one go. It allows for incremental adjustments to the Current Target Elevation, ensuring that the desired depth is reached without stalling or compromising the grading process.
Where can I find the Nudge Sensitivity setting?The Nudge Sensitivity setting can be found in the Settings Menu of the grading screen.
Does the Nudge Depth feature modify the Final Target Elevation values?No, the Nudge Depth feature does not modify the Final Target Elevation values. The original design remains unaltered in both simple grading and advanced scenarios like Slope-IQ or land forming.
How can I adjust the Current Target Elevation using the Nudge Depth feature?To adjust the Current Target Elevation, you can tap the Nudge Up or Nudge Down arrows on the grading screen. Each tap adjusts the Current Target Elevation by the amount specified in the Nudge Sensitivity setting.
Can the Nudge Depth function be used in all grading scenarios?Yes, the Nudge Depth function is applicable in any grading scenario, including implementing Slope-IQ and executing an imported land forming/leveling design. It provides flexibility in achieving the desired depth incrementally and ensures smooth progress in the grading process.
How does the Nudge Depth function work when implementing a Slope-IQ design?When implementing a Slope-IQ design, the on-screen profile graph on the grading screen can be used to view your current position relative to the design. By tapping the Nudge Up button, you can temporarily raise the Current Target Elevation, allowing you to cut higher than the finished depth. This incremental adjustment helps you complete the pass without stalling.
How does the Nudge Depth function work when executing an imported land leveling design?When executing an imported land leveling design, you can refer to the imported cut/fill map overlay. By tapping the Nudge Up button, you can raise the implement to cut above the finished grade. This incremental adjustment, specified by the Nudge Sensitivity setting, ensures that you can achieve the desired depth without interruptions.
How many times should I tap the Nudge Up button in the first pass of a Slope-IQ design?In the first pass of a Slope-IQ design, you can tap the Nudge Up button twice to temporarily raise the Current Target Elevation. Each tap adjusts the elevation by the amount specified in the Nudge Sensitivity setting. For example, if the Nudge Sensitivity is set to 2 inches, tapping the Nudge Up button twice will raise the implement by 4 inches above the finished depth.
What does it mean when the path is marked red in the Map Markers?If Map Markers are enabled in Settings, the path will be marked red to indicate that you've worked on the route but haven't achieved the final target depth. This visual indication helps you keep track of the progress and identify areas where the final depth is yet to be reached.
How should I adjust the Nudge Offset in the second pass of a grading process?In the second pass of a grading process, you should press the Nudge Down button once to decrease the nudge offset. This adjustment reduces the Current Target Elevation by the amount specified in the Nudge Sensitivity setting. By doing so, the second pass will cut slightly above the finished grade, allowing for a more precise grading operation.
What action should I take in the final pass of a grading process?In the final pass of a grading process, you should press the Nudge Down button again to set the Nudge Offset value to zero. This adjustment ensures that the implement reaches the final target grade, aligning with the desired depth specified in the grading design.
How can I ensure smooth progress in the grading process using the Nudge Depth function?By utilizing the Nudge Depth function, you can achieve smooth progress in the grading process. Adjusting the Current Target Elevation incrementally with the Nudge Up and Nudge Down buttons allows you to navigate challenging cut depths without stalling or compromising the overall grading operation. This feature provides flexibility and precision, ensuring optimal results in various grading scenarios.
Is the Nudge Depth function applicable only to advanced grading techniques?No, the Nudge Depth function is applicable to both simple grading and advanced grading techniques like Slope-IQ or land forming. Whether you're working on a basic grading task or a more complex design, the Nudge Depth function can be used to adjust the Current Target Elevation incrementally, enhancing the efficiency and accuracy of the grading process.
How can I access the Settings Menu on the grading screen?To access the Settings Menu on the grading screen, you can typically find an icon or button with a gear or wrench symbol. Tapping on this icon will open the Settings Menu, where you can find various options and configurations related to the grading process, including the Nudge Sensitivity setting.
Can the Nudge Depth function be customized based on my preferences?Yes, the Nudge Depth function can be customized to suit your preferences. The Nudge Sensitivity setting allows you to specify the amount by which the Current Target Elevation is adjusted with each tap of the Nudge Up or Nudge Down buttons. By setting the desired value, you can fine-tune the nudge increment to meet your specific requirements during the grading process.
How does the Nudge Depth function benefit the grading workflow?The Nudge Depth function provides significant benefits to the grading workflow. It allows for incremental adjustments to the Current Target Elevation, reducing the cut depth when necessary. This feature eliminates the need for multiple start points in simple grading and preserves the integrity of the original design in advanced grading scenarios. By enabling smooth progress and precise control, the Nudge Depth function enhances efficiency and accuracy throughout the grading process.
Is the Nudge Depth function available in all software versions?Yes, the Nudge Depth function is available in all compatible software versions. This feature is designed to enhance the grading process and is typically included in the functionality of the software. If you're using an older version of the software, it's recommended to update to the latest version to access the Nudge Depth function and other improvements or bug fixes.
How can I determine the appropriate Nudge Sensitivity value for my grading needs?The appropriate Nudge Sensitivity value depends on your specific grading needs and preferences. It's recommended to start with the default setting and make adjustments based on your experience and the requirements of the grading project. You can experiment with different values to find the ideal Nudge Sensitivity that provides the desired cut depth adjustments while maintaining smooth progress and accuracy in the grading process.
Does the Nudge Depth function work in real-time during the grading process?Yes, the Nudge Depth function works in real-time during the grading process. You can make adjustments to the Current Target Elevation by tapping the Nudge Up or Nudge Down buttons as needed, and the implement will respond accordingly, ensuring precise grading results. This real-time responsiveness allows for on-the-fly adjustments and adaptability during the grading operation.
How can I ensure the accuracy of the Nudge Depth adjustments?To ensure the accuracy of the Nudge Depth adjustments, it's essential to reference the on-screen profile graph or the imported cut/fill map overlay, depending on the grading scenario. By visualizing your current position relative to the design, you can make informed decisions regarding the Nudge Depth adjustments and ensure that the implement is cutting at the desired depth for optimal grading results.
What is the purpose of color-coding the lines on the map screen?The color-coded lines on the map screen help visualize the areas where the target depth has been reached or not, providing a quick and clear overview of the work performed.
What does the overlaying of new lines on the map indicate?Overlaying new lines on the map indicates retracing the same route. It helps visualize the areas where additional work was performed due to the target depth not being attained on the first pass.
How can the map display in Ditch Assist be adjusted?The map display in Ditch Assist can be adjusted by toggling the settings to show or hide the lines representing work completion.
How does Ditch Assist store survey and completed work data?Ditch Assist stores survey and completed work data directly on your device for easy access and retrieval.
Can the format for exporting data be changed in Ditch Assist?Yes, the format for exporting data can be changed in Ditch Assist through the Settings.
What are the three columns in the XYZ text format?The XYZ text format consists of three columns: X (coordinates in decimal degrees), Y (coordinates in decimal degrees), and Z (elevation in feet or meters, depending on the chosen measurement system).
How have recent Android updates affected data writing in Ditch Assist?Recent Android updates have modified data writing permissions, which can lead to crashes when the app is unable to write data to the device storage, even after obtaining the necessary permissions.
What changes have been made to the data storage directory in Ditch Assist?Starting from app version 4.20.5, data is saved to the directory: Android-Data-com.AutoBotz.DitchAssist.MC
Can the built-in file manager access the new data storage directory?No, the built-in file manager on your device cannot access the new data storage directory in Ditch Assist.
What is the recommended third-party file manager app to access the new data storage location?We recommend using Cx File Explorer, a third-party file manager app, to access the new data storage location.
What should be done to view files in the new data storage location using Cx File Explorer?To view files in the new data storage location, open Cx File Explorer and navigate to the specified directory.
What permissions might be required to access the new data storage location using Cx File Explorer?When accessing the new data storage location using Cx File Explorer for the first time, you may need to grant the app permissions to access the directory.
Why is the XYZ text format recommended for exporting data?The XYZ text format is recommended for exporting data because it provides a structured file with easily readable columns: X, Y (coordinates in decimal degrees), and Z (elevation in feet or meters). This facilitates compatibility with drainage or land level design software.
What information does the color of the lines on the map provide?The color of the lines on the map provides visual information about the achievement of the target depth. Green lines indicate successful target depth attainment, while red lines indicate areas where the target depth was not reached.
Can the display of work completion on the map be disabled?Yes, the display of work completion on the map can be disabled in the Settings of the Ditch Assist app.
What is the purpose of overlaying new lines on the map in Ditch Assist?Overlaying new lines on the map in Ditch Assist indicates retracing the same route to make additional passes in areas where the target depth was not achieved.
Can the logged data be saved in different formats in Ditch Assist?Yes, the logged data in Ditch Assist can be saved in a delimited text format (XYZ) or as a KML file, providing flexibility for exporting and compatibility with various software applications.
How have recent Android updates impacted the writing of data in Ditch Assist?Recent Android updates have modified data writing permissions, resulting in potential crashes when the app is unable to write data to the device storage, even after obtaining the necessary permissions.
What is the file storage directory for Ditch Assist starting from app version 4.20.5?Starting from app version 4.20.5, the file storage directory for Ditch Assist is Android-Data-com.AutoBotz.DitchAssist.MC
Can the built-in file manager access the file storage directory in Ditch Assist?No, the built-in file manager on your device cannot access the file storage directory used by Ditch Assist.
Which third-party file manager app is recommended for accessing the file storage directory?Cx File Explorer, a third-party file manager app, is recommended for accessing the file storage directory in Ditch Assist.
How can Cx File Explorer be used to access the file storage directory?To access the file storage directory using Cx File Explorer, install the app from the Play Store and grant it necessary permissions. Then, use the app to navigate to the directory where the data is saved.
Are there any additional permissions required when using Cx File Explorer for the first time?When using Cx File Explorer for the first time to access the file storage directory, you might need to grant the app additional permissions to access the specified location on your device.
How is data saved in Ditch Assist for survey and completed work?Ditch Assist automatically saves survey data and completed work data directly on your device for easy retrieval and analysis.
Can the export format be changed in Ditch Assist?Yes, in Ditch Assist, you can change the export format for the logged data through the Settings.
What are the three columns in the XYZ text format used for exporting data?The XYZ text format for exporting data consists of three columns: X (coordinates in decimal degrees), Y (coordinates in decimal degrees), and Z (elevation in feet or meters based on the chosen measurement system).
What issues have some users encountered with recent Android updates and data writing?Some users have experienced crashes in Ditch Assist due to recent Android updates modifying data writing permissions. These crashes occur when the app is unable to write data to the device storage, even after obtaining the necessary permissions.
How has the data storage directory changed in Ditch Assist?Starting from app version 4.20.5, the data storage directory in Ditch Assist has been changed to Android-Data-com.AutoBotz.DitchAssist.MC.
Can the default file manager access the new data storage directory?No, the default file manager on your device cannot access the new data storage directory used by Ditch Assist.
Which third-party file manager app is recommended to access the new data storage location?To access the new data storage location, we recommend using Cx File Explorer, a third-party file manager app available on the Play Store.
How can Cx File Explorer be used to view files in the new data storage location?To view files in the new data storage location using Cx File Explorer, open the app, grant the necessary permissions, and navigate to the specified directory where the data is saved.
Are there any specific permissions required to access the new data storage location using Cx File Explorer?When accessing the new data storage location using Cx File Explorer for the first time, you may need to grant the app additional permissions to access the directory where the data is saved.
What benefits does the XYZ text format offer for data export?The XYZ text format offers a structured file format with three columns: X, Y (coordinates in decimal degrees), and Z (elevation in feet or meters). This format ensures compatibility with drainage or land level design software, enabling seamless data transfer and analysis.
How can the color-coded lines on the map screen be interpreted?The color-coded lines on the map screen provide a visual representation of the work performed. Green lines indicate successful target depth attainment, while red lines indicate areas where the target depth was not reached. This allows for quick identification of areas requiring further work.
Is it possible to hide the lines representing work completion on the map?Yes, you can customize the display by hiding the lines representing work completion on the map through the app's Settings.
How does overlaying new lines on the map in Ditch Assist benefit the user?Overlaying new lines on the map allows the user to track and visualize areas where additional work has been performed due to target depth not being achieved on the initial pass. This helps in identifying specific sections that require further attention.
What options are available for exporting data in Ditch Assist?In Ditch Assist, you can export data in either a delimited text format (XYZ) or as a KML file, depending on your preferences and the requirements of the software you intend to use the data with.
What are the advantages of using a third-party file manager like Cx File Explorer?A third-party file manager like Cx File Explorer provides access to specific directories, such as the new data storage location in Ditch Assist, which may not be accessible using the device's default file manager. This allows you to manage and view files more effectively.
Can the data storage location be accessed using the built-in file manager?No, the built-in file manager on your device cannot access the data storage location used by Ditch Assist.
How can Cx File Explorer be used to access the new data storage location in Ditch Assist?To access the new data storage location in Ditch Assist using Cx File Explorer, install the app, grant it the necessary permissions, and navigate to the specified directory where the data is saved.
Do you need to grant additional permissions when using Cx File Explorer for the first time?When using Cx File Explorer for the first time to access the new data storage location, you may need to grant the app additional permissions to access the directory on your device.
Can the export format in Ditch Assist be customized?Yes, the export format in Ditch Assist can be customized based on your preferences and the requirements of the software you intend to use the exported data with.
What do recent Android updates involve regarding data writing in Ditch Assist?Recent Android updates have modified the permissions related to data writing in Ditch Assist, which can result in crashes if the app is unable to write data to the device storage even after obtaining the necessary permissions.
What is the new file storage directory for Ditch Assist starting from app version 4.20.5?Starting from app version 4.20.5, the file storage directory for Ditch Assist is Android-Data-com.AutoBotz.DitchAssist.MC.
Can the default file manager access the new file storage directory in Ditch Assist?No, the default file manager on your device cannot access the new file storage directory used by Ditch Assist.
Which third-party file manager app is recommended to access the new file storage location?To access the new file storage location, we recommend using Cx File Explorer, a third-party file manager app available on the Play Store.
How can Cx File Explorer be used to view files in the new file storage location?To view files in the new file storage location using Cx File Explorer, open the app, grant the necessary permissions, and navigate to the specified directory where the data is saved.
Are there any specific permissions required to access the new file storage location using Cx File Explorer?When accessing the new file storage location using Cx File Explorer for the first time, you may need to grant the app additional permissions to access the directory where the data is saved.
What advantages does the XYZ text format offer for data export in Ditch Assist?The XYZ text format offers a structured file format with three columns: X (coordinates in decimal degrees), Y (coordinates in decimal degrees), and Z (elevation in feet or meters). This format ensures compatibility with various software applications for data analysis and further processing.
How can the color-coded lines on the map screen be interpreted in Grading Mode?In Grading Mode, the color-coded lines on the map screen provide a visual representation of the areas where the target depth has been reached or not. Green lines indicate successful target depth attainment, while red lines indicate areas where the target depth was not achieved.
Can the display of work completion on the map be customized in Ditch Assist?Yes, in Ditch Assist, the display of work completion on the map can be customized according to user preferences.
What formats are supported for kml reference layers in Ditch Assist?Ditch Assist supports kml file overlays, which are the native format of Google Earth. You can import kml reference layers in polyline format without excessively complex geometry.
How can you import image reference layers in Ditch Assist?To import image reference layers in Ditch Assist, the images must be georeferenced in PNG or JPG formats using the EPSG: 4326 WGS84 geographic coordinate system. Each image file should have an associated world file (.pgw, .jgw). You can then import the images by selecting the image and its corresponding world file in the Manage Layers screen.
What coordinate reference system (CRS) should be used for image reference layers in Ditch Assist?The required CRS for image reference layers in Ditch Assist is Geographic WGS84. Images must be exported from your GIS application using the EPSG: 4326 WGS84 geographic coordinate system.
How can you add image overlays in Ditch Assist?To add image overlays in Ditch Assist, you need to generate the reference layers in PNG or JPG format with the associated world files (.pgw, .jgw). Then, you can import the image and world files by selecting them in the Manage Layers screen and confirming the import.
What should be considered when importing kml layers in Ditch Assist?When importing kml layers in Ditch Assist, it's important to ensure that the kml files are saved directly onto the internal memory of your device. Additionally, the kml layers should be in polyline format without excessively complex geometry to avoid freezing or slowdowns in the app.
What are the two main methods to preload or cache map imagery in the Ditch Assist App?The two main methods to preload or cache map imagery in the Ditch Assist App are accessing the app while connected to an internet-enabled Wi-Fi network and manually navigating and zooming into the area of interest, or downloading the imagery directly from the field using a tethered cell phone and the Ditch Assist Wi-Fi.
What types of files should be copied to the device for importing georeferenced image overlays in Ditch Assist?When importing georeferenced image overlays in Ditch Assist, both the image file (PNG or JPG format) and its associated world file (with .pgw or .jgw extension) should be copied to the device. Both files must be selected during the import process to ensure proper positioning of the image overlay.
Can the Ditch Assist Control Module be connected to multiple tablets simultaneously?No, the Ditch Assist Control Module can only be connected to one tablet at a time.
What should you do if the Ditch Assist App crashes on your tablet?If the Ditch Assist App crashes on your tablet, you can try updating the app to the latest version, ensuring that you have sufficient storage space on your device, or using a supported device (10" Android tablet with a recent version of the Android OS) for optimal performance.
Is the Ditch Assist Control Module's Wi-Fi network intended for internet access?No, the Ditch Assist Control Module's Wi-Fi network does not provide internet access. It is solely for connecting your tablet to the Control Module for communication and control purposes.
How can you verify the correct positioning of an image overlay in Ditch Assist?To verify the correct positioning of an image overlay in Ditch Assist, you can zoom out on the map after adding the overlay. This allows you to confirm if the overlay is accurately positioned within the context of the map.
What is the default Wi-Fi password for connecting to the Ditch Assist Control Module?The default Wi-Fi password for connecting to the Ditch Assist Control Module is "12345678".
Can I change the Wi-Fi password for the Ditch Assist Control Module?No, the Wi-Fi password for the Ditch Assist Control Module is factory set and cannot be changed.
How do I connect my tablet to the Ditch Assist Control Module's Wi-Fi network?To connect your tablet to the Ditch Assist Control Module's Wi-Fi network, go to your device's Wi-Fi settings and look for an available network beginning with "DitchAssist...". Select the network and enter the default Wi-Fi password "12345678".
Is the Ditch Assist Control Module's Wi-Fi network secure?The Ditch Assist Control Module's Wi-Fi network is secure and requires the default Wi-Fi password "12345678" for connection.
What should I do if I can't find the Ditch Assist Wi-Fi network on my tablet?If you can't find the Ditch Assist Wi-Fi network on your tablet, make sure that the Control Module is powered on and the Wi-Fi is enabled. Check your device's Wi-Fi settings again and wait for the network to appear. You may also try moving closer to the Control Module to improve the Wi-Fi signal reception.
Can I connect multiple tablets to the Ditch Assist Control Module's Wi-Fi network simultaneously?No, the Ditch Assist Control Module's Wi-Fi network allows only one tablet to be connected at a time.
What if I forget the Wi-Fi password for the Ditch Assist Control Module?If you forget the Wi-Fi password for the Ditch Assist Control Module, you can refer to the user manual or documentation provided with the system to retrieve the default password. It is set to "12345678" by the factory and cannot be changed.
Does the Ditch Assist Control Module's Wi-Fi network provide internet access?No, the Ditch Assist Control Module's Wi-Fi network does not provide internet access. It is solely intended for connecting your tablet to the Control Module for communication and control purposes.
What if I can't connect to the Ditch Assist Wi-Fi network using the default password?If you are unable to connect to the Ditch Assist Wi-Fi network using the default password "12345678," ensure that you are entering the password correctly. If the issue persists, you may try restarting the Control Module and attempting the connection again. If the problem still persists, contact our customer support team for further assistance.
Can I change the Wi-Fi network name (SSID) for the Ditch Assist Control Module?No, the Wi-Fi network name (SSID) for the Ditch Assist Control Module is factory set and cannot be changed. Look for a network beginning with "DitchAssist..." in your device's Wi-Fi settings to connect to the Control Module.
What is the purpose of the Ditch Assist Control Module's Wi-Fi network?The Ditch Assist Control Module's Wi-Fi network is used to establish a connection between the Control Module and your tablet, allowing for communication and control of the Ditch Assist system through the Ditch Assist App.
Do I need an internet connection to connect to the Ditch Assist Control Module's Wi-Fi network?No, an internet connection is not required to connect to the Ditch Assist Control Module's Wi-Fi network. The network is solely for establishing a direct connection between the Control Module and your tablet for communication and control purposes.
What should I do if I experience Wi-Fi connectivity issues with the Ditch Assist Control Module?If you experience Wi-Fi connectivity issues with the Ditch Assist Control Module, ensure that you are within range of the Control Module and there are no physical obstructions that may interfere with the signal. You can also try restarting both the Control Module and your tablet. If the problem persists, contact our customer support team for further assistance.
Can I connect to the Ditch Assist Wi-Fi network using multiple devices simultaneously?The Ditch Assist Wi-Fi network allows only one device to be connected at a time. If you want to connect another device, ensure that the previous device is disconnected first.
What should I do if I encounter a Wi-Fi password error when connecting to the Ditch Assist Control Module?If you encounter a Wi-Fi password error when connecting to the Ditch Assist Control Module, double-check that you have entered the default Wi-Fi password "12345678" correctly. If the issue persists, restart both the Control Module and your tablet, and try the connection again. If you continue to experience problems, contact our customer support team for assistance.
Can I change the default Wi-Fi password for the Ditch Assist Control Module to a personalized password?No, the default Wi-Fi password for the Ditch Assist Control Module cannot be changed to a personalized password. It is set to "12345678" by the factory to ensure security and compatibility with the Ditch Assist system.
Is the Ditch Assist Wi-Fi network visible to other nearby devices?The Ditch Assist Wi-Fi network is visible to nearby devices that are scanning for available networks. However, the network is secure and requires the default Wi-Fi password "12345678" for connection.
What do I do if I encounter a "Wi-Fi network not found" error when connecting to the Ditch Assist Control Module?If you encounter a "Wi-Fi network not found" error when connecting to the Ditch Assist Control Module, make sure that the Control Module is powered on and the Wi-Fi is enabled. Also, check your tablet's Wi-Fi settings and ensure that you are within range of the Control Module. If the problem persists, try restarting both the Control Module and your tablet before attempting the connection again.
Can I reset the Wi-Fi password for the Ditch Assist Control Module?No, the Wi-Fi password for the Ditch Assist Control Module cannot be reset or changed. It remains as the default password "12345678" set by the factory.
What is the range of the Ditch Assist Wi-Fi network?The range of the Ditch Assist Wi-Fi network depends on various factors, including environmental conditions and obstructions. Generally, the Wi-Fi range can extend up to several tens of meters in an open outdoor area. However, it's recommended to be within a reasonable proximity to the Control Module for optimal signal strength and stability.
What will Ditch Assist document while operating in Grading Mode?While operating in Grading Mode, Ditch Assist will document the work performed locations as colored lines on the map screen. These lines will be color-coded to indicate whether the target depth has been reached.
How are the lines on the map screen color-coded?The lines on the map screen are color-coded. Green lines indicate that the implement has successfully reached the target depth, while red lines indicate areas where the target depth was not achieved.
Can the feature to display work completion on the map be turned off?Yes, the feature to display work completion on the map can be toggled on or off in the Settings.
What happens when retracing the same route in Grading Mode?When retracing the same route, new lines will be overlaid on top of the old ones to indicate the work performed. These new lines will also be color-coded based on the achieved target depth.
What can the colored lines on the map screen help identify?The colored lines on the map screen can help identify areas requiring further work. If green lines are layered over red ones, it signifies that the target depth has been reached, indicating that you can proceed to the next section.
How does Ditch Assist log survey and completed work data?Ditch Assist automatically logs both survey data and completed work data, storing it directly on your device.
How can you save the logged data in Ditch Assist?You can choose to save the logged data in Ditch Assist in a delimited text format (XYZ) or as a KML file through the Settings.
What format is recommended for exporting data for drainage or land level design software?For exporting data to drainage or land level design software, we suggest using the XYZ text format. This format produces a text file with three columns: X, Y (coordinates in decimal degrees), and Z (elevation in feet or meters, depending on your chosen measurement system).
What changes have recent Android updates made to data writing permissions?Recent Android updates have modified the permissions regarding how data (such as survey and ditching log files) can be written to the device storage.
What issues have some users experienced due to these changes in permissions?Some users have experienced crashes when the Ditch Assist app is unable to write data to the device storage, even after obtaining the necessary permissions during the initial app setup.
Can the built-in file manager on the device access the saved data directory?No, the built-in file manager on the device cannot access the directory where the data is saved.
What third-party file manager app is recommended to access the saved data directory?We recommend installing a third-party file manager app like Cx File Explorer from the Play Store.
How can Cx File Explorer be used to access the saved data location?You can use Cx File Explorer to navigate to the file location by opening the app and granting the necessary permissions to access the directory.
Are there any additional considerations when using Cx File Explorer for the first time?When using Cx File Explorer for the first time to access the saved data location, you might need to grant the app permissions to access the directory.
What is the purpose of the XYZ text format for exporting data?The purpose of the XYZ text format is to provide a structured text file with three columns: X, Y (coordinates in decimal degrees), and Z (elevation in feet or meters). This format facilitates the compatibility of data with drainage or land level design software.
What is the significance of green lines on the map screen in Grading Mode?In Grading Mode, green lines on the map screen indicate that the implement has successfully reached the target depth at those locations.
How can red lines on the map screen in Grading Mode be interpreted?Red lines on the map screen in Grading Mode indicate areas where the target depth was not achieved by the implement.
Can the display of work completion on the map be customized?Yes, the display of work completion on the map can be customized through the Settings.
Where should the large Deutsch connectors on the main harness be plugged into the Control Module?The large Deutsch connectors on the main harness should be plugged into ports A and C of the Ditch Assist Control Module. Ports B and D should be left vacant and sealed with dust plugs.
How should the power harness be connected during installation?The power harness should be linked directly to the tractor battery terminals and connected to the power connector on the main harness. It should only be connected to the vehicle battery, not a trailer plug or in-cab power strip.
How should the GPS breakout cable be routed during installation?The GPS breakout cable should be routed from the open connector on the harness to the GPS antenna location, ensuring proper cable management and protection from potential damage.
What should be done to prevent damage to components during equipment operation?Ensure that all connectors are securely plugged, ports are sealed when not in use, and cables and hoses are properly secured to prevent damage to Ditch Assist components during equipment operation.
Can the Control Module be installed on any suitable spot on the equipment?Yes, the Control Module can be securely installed either at the implement's front, near the hitch, or at the tractor's rear, as long as it provides a clear line of sight to the cab and minimal obstructions.
How should the proportional valve be positioned during installation?The proportional valve should be securely installed in a location that allows the hydraulic hoses to connect to it and enables the attached hoses to reach the tractor's rear ports.
Can the proportional valve be permanently mounted on the tractor?Yes, if you intend to use Ditch Assist with multiple implements, it is suggested to permanently mount the valve on the tractor for convenience.
What precautions should be taken when mounting the valve bracket on a scraper or land leveler?The valve bracket should be securely mounted to the implement, ensuring it won't interfere with moving parts or hydraulic lines. Remove the module before welding anything to the implement to prevent damage.
How many hydraulic hoses need to be connected to the tractor?Four hydraulic hoses need to be connected to the tractor: two from the P and T ports on the valve and two for the bypass.
What should be adjusted if the implement moves too quickly or too slowly?Depending on the tractor's age and size, you may need to adjust the flow rate of the hydraulic remotes to achieve the desired speed of implement movement.
How should the wiring harness be connected to the proportional valve?Attach the two plugs from the harness to the connectors on the proportional valve, ensuring correct orientation. The connectors may need to be swapped if manual controls in the app work in reverse.
Where should the large Deutsch connectors on the main harness be plugged into the Control Module?The large Deutsch connectors on the main harness should be plugged into ports A and C of the Ditch Assist Control Module. Ports B and D should be left vacant and sealed with dust plugs.
How should the power harness be connected during installation?The power harness should be linked directly to the tractor battery terminals and connected to the power connector on the main harness. It should only be connected to the vehicle battery, not a trailer plug or in-cab power strip.
How should the GPS breakout cable be routed during installation?The GPS breakout cable should be routed from the open connector on the harness to the GPS antenna location, ensuring proper cable management and protection from potential damage.
