Have some users been satisfied with lower accuracy correction systems?Yes, some users have chosen to use lower accuracy systems and have been satisfied with the performance, although it is not endorsed.
What is the requirement for mounting the GPS antenna with Ditch Assist?The GPS antenna must be mounted on the implement to be controlled and attached to a part of the implement that moves up and down in proportion to the cutting edge or blade.
What will happen if the GPS antenna is not mounted correctly?If the GPS antenna is not mounted correctly, the operation of Ditch Assist will be incorrect.
How should the GPS antenna be positioned to avoid signal blockage?The GPS antenna should be mounted in a raised position to avoid signal blockage, sitting above any objects that may cause the satellite signal to be blocked or deflected.
How can a metal object near the GPS antenna affect accuracy?A metal object near the GPS antenna can cause the incoming GPS signals to bounce off it, leading to decreased GPS accuracy.
Is it important to consider the working position of the GPS antenna?Yes, it is important to consider the working position of the GPS antenna, as the working position may be considerably lower than the transport position.
What might be required for the GPS antenna's mounting position?A taller mast may be required for the GPS antenna's mounting position, depending on the height difference between the working position and the transport position.
How does the accuracy of GPS affect the performance of Ditch Assist?The accuracy of GPS directly affects the performance of Ditch Assist.
What is the minimum requirement for the vertical accuracy of a correction service?The minimum requirement for the vertical accuracy of a correction service is 2-3 inches.
What types of correction signals are not compatible with Ditch Assist?Ditch Assist is not compatible with low-accuracy correction signals like WAAS or low-accuracy SBAS corrections such as OmniSTAR L1.
Which type of correction signal is recommended for Ditch Assist?Ditch Assist recommends using a subscription-based dual-frequency (L1/L2) satellite-delivered correction signal.
What should be kept in mind when using cautionary correction services?When using cautionary correction services, slight fluctuations in elevation readings may occur, even when the machine is stationary.
Why are cautionary correction services not suitable for pancake-flat fields?Cautionary correction services are not suitable for pancake-flat fields because they may not provide accurate results in such terrain.
What are the recommended correction methods for surface grading?The recommended correction methods for surface grading are Network RTK Correction through CORS, Cellular, or Long Range Radio link to a remote base station.
What is Ditch Assist and how can it help me?Ditch Assist is an advanced automatic machine control system that can make your work like surface drainage, land leveling, and simple tile drainage installation much easier. You can control it right from your Android tablet and it works with all sorts of machines such as scrapers, ditchers, and land levelers. It doesn't matter how old or what brand your tractor is, Ditch Assist can work with it. It helps you to manage your land better, giving you full control of your projects.
How do I operate Ditch Assist?You can control Ditch Assist using an Android App on a standard 10" tablet. The app has a simple interface that lets you operate the system remotely, making your work more convenient.
Can Ditch Assist help me with basic drainage, grading, and leveling?Yes, Ditch Assist is like your laser guidance system but with the extra benefits of GPS technology. All you need to do is set the cutting edge of the implement to the ground at a specific start point, tell the system the grade you want, and begin your work from there. Ditch Assist will do the rest, guiding you on the screen or controlling your machine directly.
What is Slope-IQ and how can it help me?Slope-IQ is an advanced feature of Ditch Assist. It allows you to survey a planned route and design the best drain to match the existing landscape, reducing the amount of work you need to do and making the most of the land's natural contours. It also allows you to design your own solution based on target points you draw on the screen, giving you full control over your work.
How can Ditch Assist help me with small fields or areas?With Slope-IQ, you can use Ditch Assist to create multi-slope planes, which is handy for making sure water drains from small areas like small plantation fields or lands meant for flood irrigation.
Can Ditch Assist work with other software?Yes, Ditch Assist can work with 3D designs from other software or service providers like OptiSurface, Topcon’s AgForm3D, and EZ Grade. Once you've created a design in the other software, you can load it into the Ditch Assist app and it'll adjust your machine's movements to match the design. This can save you a lot of time and money.
What is this PWM valve I keep hearing about?The PWM valve is a key part of Ditch Assist's automation. This special valve, which is part of the Full Automation Kit, allows precise control of your implement's movements. This means you can get highly accurate grading and drainage with less effort.
Can I use Ditch Assist on any tractor?Yes, one of the great things about Ditch Assist is that it can work with any tractor, no matter its age, make, or model. It doesn't need any specific systems to work, and it can control any implement like scrapers, ditchers, blades, and levelers. So you can get started right away with the machinery you already have.
What exactly is a 'grade' and how does Ditch Assist help manage it?A 'grade' refers to the desired slope or level of your land. Ditch Assist helps you manage this by using GPS technology to calculate the necessary target elevation based on your input and guides the machine to achieve this precise grade, whether you're aiming for a flat level surface or a particular slope.
Can Ditch Assist help me deal with water pooling in my plantation fields?Yes, definitely! Ditch Assist can help you create multi-slope planes using the Slope-IQ feature. It's a great way to make sure all water drains properly from your fields, preventing water pooling.
How does Ditch Assist help with my surface or subsurface drains?Ditch Assist allows you to construct simple surface or subsurface drains. By using RTK GPS, Ditch Assist ensures precision that's equal to laser-guided systems, without the hassle of laser transmitter repositioning or interference from environmental conditions.
What happens if I want to adjust the design mid-way through a project?With the advanced Slope-IQ Customizer, you can adjust the system to suit your specific needs anytime. You have complete control over your job, so if you need to change something mid-way, Ditch Assist can handle it.
How can Ditch Assist help improve my farm's efficiency?By providing automated machine control for tasks like surface drainage, land leveling, and tile drainage installation, Ditch Assist helps you manage your land better. It simplifies these tasks, which not only saves you time but also improves accuracy and efficiency, allowing you to get more done on your farm with less effort.
What is a 'best-fit solution' that Slope-IQ provides?A 'best-fit solution' means that Slope-IQ calculates the optimal drain design by considering the existing landscape and your specified minimum grade. It follows natural contours where there's enough slope, which means less material to move and maximum use of the existing terrain.
Can I use Ditch Assist if I don't have internet in my field?Absolutely! Ditch Assist operates using GPS technology, not the internet. So as long as you have a GPS signal, you can use Ditch Assist in your field without any worries.
How can Ditch Assist help me with basic drainage, grading, and leveling tasks?Ditch Assist makes these tasks simpler by providing precise control. You just need to set your implement at a specific starting point, input the desired grade, and start your work. Ditch Assist will calculate the necessary target elevation and either guide you with on-screen arrows or directly control the implement's up/down hydraulics, depending on your kit.
I need to create a complex drainage system on my land. Can Ditch Assist help with that?Yes, with the advanced Slope-IQ feature, Ditch Assist allows you to create complex surface or subsurface drains. After surveying your proposed route, you can use Slope-IQ to design the best solution that aligns with your existing topography and maintains your specified minimum grade.
How can Ditch Assist help me with land forming or leveling?Ditch Assist can help implement advanced 3D land forming or leveling designs using files from software providers like OptiSurface, AgForm3D, and Ezigrade. Once you load the design into the Ditch Assist app, the system will calculate the intended design elevation at all locations and adjust the implement hydraulics accordingly, ensuring high precision.
I have a small field that needs flood irrigation. Is Ditch Assist suitable for this?Absolutely! Ditch Assist is excellent for smaller areas where you need to implement multi slope planes, such as for flood irrigation. By creating a design and working outward from a central survey route, you can ensure proper water drainage across the entire field.
Can Ditch Assist support my existing machinery, even if it's old?Yes, Ditch Assist is designed to be versatile. It works with any implement, regardless of the age, make, or model of your tractor. So, no matter what machinery you have, Ditch Assist can help improve its functionality.
Who owns the Ditch Assist software?Northern Plains Drainage Systems Ltd, the Vendor, retains all intellectual property rights, including copyright and distribution rights, for the Ditch Assist software. This license does not transfer any ownership rights of the Software to the user or Licensee.
Can I modify or reverse-engineer the Ditch Assist software?No, the Licensee agrees not to modify, reverse-engineer, or decompile the Software. Any violation of these terms can be considered a substantial breach of the Agreement and may lead to its termination.
What happens if the Vendor introduces new features to the Software after my purchase?If new features are introduced that increase the price of the Software, the Vendor may offer the Licensee an opportunity to access these features as an upgrade for an additional cost.
What liabilities does the Vendor assume for the Software?The Vendor provides the Software "as is" and does not assume liability for any direct, indirect, incidental, or consequential damages that the Licensee may suffer due to the use or failure to use the Software.
What happens if I fail to comply with the terms of the Agreement?If the Licensee fails to comply with any terms of the Agreement, the Agreement may be terminated.
What warranty does the Vendor provide for the hardware components of Ditch Assist?The Vendor guarantees that for a duration of 12 months from the purchase date, all hardware components will be free from defects in materials and workmanship when used normally, in accordance with the product's intended usage and installation.
What happens if a hardware component fails during the warranty period?If a hardware component fails during the 12-month warranty period, the Vendor commits to repairing or replacing the faulty hardware component at no cost to the Licensee.
Does the Vendor provide a warranty for damages caused by misuse or natural disasters?No, the Vendor does not provide a warranty for damages caused by misuse, alterations, negligence, accidents, vandalism, natural disasters, incorrect installation, or improper storage.
What is the Vendor's responsibility regarding future Android OS releases and hardware compatibility?The Vendor commits to providing updates and maintaining the Software to remain compatible with future hardware and Android OS releases as per industry standards. However, the Vendor cannot guarantee that the Software will always be compatible with all future hardware and Android OS releases, and will not be held responsible for any losses or damages resulting from such incompatibility.
Where should I send notifications to the Vendor under this Agreement?All notifications to the Vendor under this Agreement should be sent to Northern Plains Drainage Systems Ltd, Box 9, Elm Creek, Manitoba, R0G 0N0, Canada.
What does this "Control Module" thing do for my Ditch Assist?The Control Module is the central component of the Ditch Assist system. It links all parts together, powers the GPS receiver, processes GPS messages, and sends/receives information wirelessly to and from the Ditch Assist Android app. It also manages the proportional valve for automatic control when in use.
Can this module handle the heat in the summer and cold in the winter?Yes, the Control Module is built to withstand a wide range of temperatures, from as cold as -40°C to as hot as +85°C. Its casing is environmentally sealed and rated IP67, which means it's highly resistant to dust and water.
I've got a 24V system on my rig. Will the Ditch Assist Control Module work with it?Absolutely! The Control Module is compatible with both 12V and 24V systems. It can efficiently operate across a voltage range of 9V to 32V.
I heard something about this module managing a "proportional valve". What does that mean?The proportional valve controls the flow of hydraulic fluid based on the input it receives. When the Control Module is in use, it smartly manages this valve to ensure the automatic control of your Ditch Assist system.
What's this "IP67" rating that the Control Module has?An IP67 rating means that the Control Module is protected against dust ingress and can withstand being submerged in water up to a meter deep for up to 30 minutes. It ensures that the Control Module is robust enough to handle tough environmental conditions.
Will the Control Module work if I've got a low battery?The Control Module is designed to operate efficiently across a voltage range of 9V to 32V. However, a severely low battery might affect its performance. It's best to ensure your equipment's battery is well-maintained for optimal performance.
What is the Ditch Assist Control Module?The Ditch Assist Control Module is the central unit of the Ditch Assist system, linking all components together. It powers the GPS receiver, processes GPS messages, facilitates wireless transmission of information to and from the Ditch Assist Android app, and manages the proportional valve for automated control.
How durable is the Ditch Assist Control Module?The Ditch Assist Control Module is built with an environmentally sealed casing rated IP67, ensuring its robustness in a wide range of operating temperatures from -40°C to +85°C.
What kind of systems is the Control Module compatible with?The Control Module is compatible with both 12V and 24V systems, and operates efficiently across a voltage range of 9V to 32V.
What communications does the Control Module support?This module supports communications via CAN bus and RS232 protocols, and also features Wi-Fi wireless capability.
What is the Ditch Assist Main Harness?The Ditch Assist Main Harness interfaces with the Control Module, coordinating with both the GPS and the proportional valve. It comes with a detachable power harness for convenient installation, and includes a connector specifically designed for a GPS breakout.
What does the GPS Breakout Harness do?The GPS Breakout Harness enables seamless connection and power supply to your GPS receiver. It is designed to interface with a wide range of agricultural GPS receivers and also includes a DB9 connector for third-party devices.
What is the purpose of the Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) Hydraulic Valve?The PWM Hydraulic Valve is essential for automated hydraulic control in the Ditch Assist system. It is custom-assembled for use with the system, and is adjustable to either open or closed center configurations.
What does the Valve Bypass Kit do?The Valve Bypass Kit allows you to integrate two extra lines into the tractor's remotes, enabling manual control and easy switching between manual and automated control without disconnecting the valve.
What is the Ditch Assist App?The Ditch Assist App is an Android application that provides an intuitive user interface for the Ditch Assist system. It is regularly updated and available for free download from the Play Store.
What is the role of the GNSS (GPS) Antenna?The GNSS (GPS) Antenna, mounted on the implement being controlled, delivers necessary NMEA position messages to the Ditch Assist Control Module, ensuring precise operation within your system.
What kind of device is required for running the Ditch Assist App?The Ditch Assist App is designed to operate on 10" Android tablets with a recent version of the Android operating system. While it can run on phones and smaller tablets, it is recommended to use a 10" or larger screen tablet for optimal performance.
What is the current tablet recommendation for running the Ditch Assist App?As of now, the recommended tablet for running the Ditch Assist App is the Samsung Galaxy Tab S6 Lite.
What happens if I use a device with a different screen resolution to run the Ditch Assist App?While the app can function on other devices and various screen resolutions, the layout may not align as intended, potentially affecting the user experience.
Can I use any valve with the Ditch Assist system?No, only valves provided by Ditch Assist authorized dealers should be used. Use of alternative valves may void the support or warranty.
Can I install the Ditch Assist Main Harness on multiple tractors?Yes, the Main Harness comes with a detachable power harness, allowing the flexibility to install multiple power harnesses on various tractors as needed.
Can I download older versions of the Ditch Assist App?Yes, older versions of the Ditch Assist App can be accessed on the App Releases page of the Ditch Assist website.
What happens if I use a cheaper Android tablet for the Ditch Assist App?Some budget-friendly units might compromise on the quality of components like Wi-Fi transmitters and available memory resources, which could lead to sub-optimal performance of the app.
Can I use my existing GNSS equipment with Ditch Assist?Yes, Ditch Assist is compatible with a wide range of popular GNSS equipment, allowing you to integrate it with your existing technology.
What is the advantage of the Valve Bypass Kit?The Valve Bypass Kit provides the advantage of swiftly switching to manual control without resorting to the manual raise/lower buttons on the application interface, eliminating the need to disconnect the valve.
Can I use the Ditch Assist system in harsh weather conditions?Yes, the Ditch Assist Control Module is designed with an environmentally sealed casing rated IP67, ensuring its robustness in a wide range of operating temperatures from -40°C to +85°C.
Can I use the Ditch Assist App on my phone?While the Ditch Assist App can technically run on phones, it's recommended to use a 10" or larger screen tablet for optimal performance, as phones and smaller tablets may encounter issues with resizing, layout, possible crashes, and missing features.
How can I update the Ditch Assist App?Updates to the Ditch Assist App are regularly published on the Play Store, facilitating seamless installation.
Can I manually control the system using the Valve Bypass Kit?Yes, the Valve Bypass Kit lets you bypass the valve when necessary, enabling exclusively manual control when needed.
What type of devices does the GPS Breakout Harness interface with?The GPS Breakout Harness interfaces with most widely-used agricultural GPS receivers and Emlid. It also includes a DB9 cable to interface with numerous other devices using manufacturer-specific breakout harnesses.
Can I use a tablet other than Samsung Galaxy Tab S6 Lite for the Ditch Assist App?While the Samsung Galaxy Tab S6 Lite is the current recommendation, the app can run on other Android tablets. However, layout and user experience might be affected depending on the screen resolution and size.
Is there a restriction on using different types of hydraulic valves with the Ditch Assist system?Yes, for automated hydraulic control, a PWM hydraulic valve is essential. Only valves offered by Ditch Assist authorized dealers should be used. No support or warranty will be extended if alternative valves are used.
How is the proportional valve in the Ditch Assist system adaptable?The proportional valve comes standard with hydraulic couplings and hoses to facilitate connection of your implement's up/down hydraulic hoses to the valve, and subsequently, the valve to the tractor remotes. Valves can be adjusted to either open or closed center configurations, enhancing their adaptability to various system requirements.
Can I use the Ditch Assist system with other GPS receivers?Yes, Ditch Assist is compatible with a wide range of popular agricultural GPS receivers and also includes a DB9 cable to interface with numerous other devices using manufacturer-specific breakout harnesses.
Why is the Ditch Assist App primarily recommended for use on tablets?The Ditch Assist App is primarily recommended for use on tablets because it is designed to run optimally on 10" Android tablets. Smaller screens like those on phones might encounter issues with resizing, layout, potential crashes, and missing features.
Can the Ditch Assist Control Module communicate wirelessly?Yes, the Ditch Assist Control Module features Wi-Fi wireless capability, which facilitates wireless transmission and reception of information to and from the Ditch Assist Android app.
Where can I download the Ditch Assist App?The Ditch Assist App can be downloaded for free from the Google Play Store. You can also access the current version of the app, as well as legacy and current beta versions, on the App Releases page of the Ditch Assist website.
What role does the GPS Breakout Harness play in the Ditch Assist system?The GPS Breakout Harness plays a crucial role in the Ditch Assist system by providing seamless connection and power supply to your specific brand of GPS receiver.
Can I permanently install the Ditch Assist Main Harness?Yes, the Ditch Assist Main Harness comes with a detachable power harness, providing the convenience of permanent installation.
Does the Control Module manage the proportional valve?Yes, the Control Module smartly manages the proportional valve for automated control when the valve is in use.
Can I use the Ditch Assist Control Module with a 24V system?Yes, the Control Module is compatible with both 12V and 24V systems, and operates efficiently across a voltage range of 9V to 32V.
What is the operating temperature range for the Ditch Assist Control Module?The operating temperature range for the Ditch Assist Control Module is from -40°C to +85°C.
Does the Ditch Assist Main Harness have a specific connector for a GPS breakout?Yes, the Ditch Assist Main Harness includes a connector specifically designed for a GPS breakout.
Is there an official valve provided for the Ditch Assist system?Yes, the official valves provided with Ditch Assist are custom-assembled exclusively for use with our system. Only valves offered by authorized dealers should be used.
What is the advantage of the Valve Bypass Kit in the Ditch Assist system?The Valve Bypass Kit lets you integrate two extra lines into the tractor's remotes, enabling you to circumvent the valve when necessary. This function can prove beneficial when there's a need for exclusively manual control.
Is the Ditch Assist app available for free?Yes, the Ditch Assist App is available for free download from the Google Play Store.
Which tablet is currently recommended for the Ditch Assist app?The current tablet recommendation for the Ditch Assist app is the Samsung Galaxy Tab S6 Lite.
Is the Ditch Assist Control Module waterproof?Yes, the Ditch Assist Control Module is designed with an environmentally sealed casing rated IP67, making it robust and waterproof.
Can the Ditch Assist Control Module work with both CAN bus and RS232 protocols?Yes, the Ditch Assist Control Module supports communications via CAN bus and RS232 protocols.
Can I use the Ditch Assist system with my existing GNSS equipment?Yes, Ditch Assist is compatible with a wide range of popular GNSS equipment, giving you the flexibility to integrate it with your existing technology.
What is the function of the GNSS Antenna in the Ditch Assist system?The GNSS antenna, mounted on the implement being controlled, delivers necessary NMEA position messages to the Ditch Assist Control Module. This process ensures precise operation and coordination within your system.
Can I use any Android tablet with the Ditch Assist App?While the Ditch Assist App can function on various Android devices, it is specifically designed to operate optimally on 10" Android tablets with a recent version of the Android operating system. Use on smaller screens may encounter issues with resizing, layout, potential crashes, and missing features.
What are the power requirements of the Ditch Assist Control Module?The Ditch Assist Control Module is compatible with both 12V and 24V systems, and operates efficiently across a voltage range of 9V to 32V.
Can I use the Ditch Assist system with Emlid GPS receivers?Yes, Ditch Assist provides GPS harnesses compatible with most widely-used agricultural GPS receivers and Emlid.
What happens if I use an alternative hydraulic valve with the Ditch Assist system?If you use an alternative hydraulic valve with the Ditch Assist system, no support or warranty will be extended.
What type of cable is included in the GPS Breakout Harness for interfacing with other devices?The GPS Breakout Harness includes a DB9 cable for interfacing with numerous other devices using manufacturer-specific breakout harnesses.
Can the Ditch Assist Main Harness accommodate multiple power harnesses on various tractors?Yes, the Ditch Assist Main Harness comes with a detachable power harness, providing the convenience of permanent installation or the flexibility to install multiple power harnesses on various tractors as needed.
Can I operate the Ditch Assist system manually when needed?Yes, by using the Valve Bypass Kit, you can integrate two extra lines into the tractor's remotes, giving you the ability to circumvent the valve when necessary. This function allows for manual control when needed.
Is the Ditch Assist Control Module resistant to extreme temperatures?Yes, the Ditch Assist Control Module is designed with an environmentally sealed casing rated IP67, ensuring its robustness in a wide range of operating temperatures from -40°C to +85°C.
Can I download the Ditch Assist app for free?Yes, the Ditch Assist App can be easily downloaded for free from the Google Play Store, by simply searching for "Ditch Assist".
What is the purpose of the Ditch Assist Main Harness?The Ditch Assist Main Harness is a crucial component that interfaces seamlessly with the Control Module, coordinating with both the GPS and the proportional valve. It also includes a connector specifically designed for a GPS breakout, permitting the connection of brand-specific GPS cables.
Where can I access updates to the Ditch Assist App?Updates to the Ditch Assist App are regularly published on the Google Play Store. You can also access the current, legacy, and beta versions of the app on the App Releases page of the Ditch Assist website at https://www.ditchassist.com/app-releases/.
How does the Ditch Assist Control Module communicate wirelessly?The Ditch Assist Control Module features Wi-Fi wireless capability, adding an extra layer of connectivity and ease of use.
Can the Ditch Assist system be operated using a phone?While it's possible to run the Ditch Assist App on phones, you may encounter issues with resizing, layout, possible crashes, and missing features. Thus, using a 10" or larger screen tablet from a recognized manufacturer is strongly recommended.
What does the Ditch Assist Control Module do?The Control Module is the central nexus of the Ditch Assist system, linking all components together. It powers the GPS receiver, gathers and processes incoming GPS messages, facilitates wireless transmission and reception of information to and from the Ditch Assist Android app, and manages the proportional valve for automated control when the valve is in use.
Are there any manufacturer-specific breakout harnesses in the GPS Breakout Harness?Yes, the GPS Breakout Harness includes a DB9 connector designed to relay GPS messages to third-party devices using manufacturer-specific breakout harnesses.
What is Ditch Assist designed for?Ditch Assist is an automatic machine control system specifically designed to transform operations in surface drainage, land leveling, land forming, and simple tile drainage installation.
Which types of machines can Ditch Assist manage?Ditch Assist can manage the raise and lower hydraulics on a variety of machines such as scrapers, ditchers, land levelers, and blades, among others.
How can Ditch Assist be operated?Ditch Assist can be operated remotely via a user-friendly interface on a standard 10" Android tablet using the Ditch Assist Android App.
What makes the Ditch Assist's Universal Grade Control unique?The Universal Grade Control, facilitated by a custom-designed PWM valve, ensures precise control of any implement, irrespective of the age, make, or model of your tractor.
Can Ditch Assist integrate with any tractor?Yes, Ditch Assist can integrate with any tractor, regardless of its age, make, or model.
Does Ditch Assist support any specific brand of GPS?Ditch Assist is GPS-ready and works with any brand of GPS, making use of a low-cost RTK or your existing RTK.
How does Ditch Assist manage grade control?Ditch Assist offers seamless, fully automated grade control through a PWM valve, with the option of manual operation using a guidance-only kit.
What customization options does Ditch Assist offer?Ditch Assist allows for full customization of ditch, tile, and plane designs via Slope-IQ, and supports the import of land forming designs. It also offers a Slope-IQ Design Customizer for more personalized system adjustments.
Can Ditch Assist implement complex land leveling designs?Yes, Ditch Assist can implement complex land leveling designs using advanced 3D designs in XYZ text format from service providers such as OptiSurface, AgForm3D, and Ezigrade.
How can Ditch Assist impact farming operations?Ditch Assist can revolutionize farming operations by providing a comprehensive, user-friendly, and cost-effective automatic machine control system, boosting efficiency and optimizing water management and land forming.
What kind of technology does Ditch Assist utilize?Ditch Assist utilizes the power of wireless technology with the Ditch Assist Android App for remote operation.
Can Ditch Assist work with older machinery?Yes, Ditch Assist takes versatility to a new level with our Universal Grade Control, which ensures precise control of any implement, irrespective of the age, make, or model of your tractor.
How does Ditch Assist offer flexibility in land forming designs?Ditch Assist supports the import of land forming designs, offering you complete control over your projects.
Is Ditch Assist compatible with all GPS brands?Yes, Ditch Assist is GPS-ready and works with any brand of GPS.
What is the PWM valve used for in Ditch Assist?In Ditch Assist, the PWM valve offers seamless, fully automated grade control with the option of manual operation using a guidance-only kit.
Does Ditch Assist allow for customization?Yes, with the innovative Slope-IQ Design Customizer, Ditch Assist lets you personalize the system to suit your specific needs.
How can Ditch Assist optimize farming operations?Ditch Assist optimizes farming operations by providing a user-friendly, comprehensive, and cost-effective automatic machine control system for surface drainage, land leveling, land forming, and simple tile drainage installation.
What services can Ditch Assist interface with for implementing complex land leveling designs?Ditch Assist can interface with services such as OptiSurface, AgForm3
Can Ditch Assist manage different types of implements?Yes, Ditch Assist supports any implement including scrapers, ditchers, blades, and levelers.
What kind of devices can run the Ditch Assist Android App?The Ditch Assist Android App is designed to operate on a standard 10" Android tablet.
How does Ditch Assist facilitate better water management?By offering fully customizable ditch, tile, and plane designs via Slope-IQ, Ditch Assist facilitates better water management.
Is Ditch Assist suitable for use with older tractors?Yes, Ditch Assist is designed to integrate with any tractor, regardless of its age, make, or model.
How is Ditch Assist future-ready?Ditch Assist is future-ready with its advanced features such as fully customizable ditch, tile, and plane designs via Slope-IQ and the ability to support the import of land forming designs.
Does Ditch Assist need CAN or ISO to integrate with a tractor?No, Ditch Assist can integrate with any tractor without the need for CAN or ISO.
How does Ditch Assist impact efficiency on the farm?Ditch Assist boosts efficiency on the farm by providing a comprehensive, user-friendly, and cost-effective automatic machine control system, enabling optimal water management, seamless land forming, and efficient operation of various implements.
Can Ditch Assist support multiple types of drainage installation?Yes, Ditch Assist is designed to handle surface drainage, land leveling, land forming, and simple tile drainage installation.
What type of device is recommended for the Ditch Assist Android App?It is recommended to use a standard 10" Android tablet for the Ditch Assist Android App.
What is the role of the custom-designed PWM valve in Ditch Assist's Universal Grade Control?The custom-designed PWM valve in Ditch Assist's Universal Grade Control feature ensures precise control of any implement, regardless of the age, make, or model of the tractor.
How does Ditch Assist make land forming more effective?Ditch Assist supports the import of land forming designs and facilitates fully customizable ditch, tile, and plane designs via Slope-IQ, thereby making land forming more effective.
How does Ditch Assist improve operations on a farm?Ditch Assist improves operations on a farm by providing a comprehensive and cost-effective automatic machine control system that can handle a variety of tasks such as surface drainage, land leveling, land forming, and simple tile drainage installation.
Can Ditch Assist be used with a variety of implements?Yes, Ditch Assist supports any implement, including scrapers, ditchers, blades, and levelers.
How can I control Ditch Assist?Ditch Assist can be controlled remotely via a user-friendly interface on a standard 10" Android tablet using the Ditch Assist Android App.
How is Ditch Assist compatible with GPS systems?Ditch Assist is GPS-ready and works with any brand of GPS, utilizing either a low-cost RTK or your existing RTK.
Can Ditch Assist offer manual operation?Yes, Ditch Assist offers the option of manual operation using a guidance-only kit along with automated grade control through its PWM valve.
How does Ditch Assist support customization of land forming designs?Ditch Assist supports the import of land forming designs and also facilitates fully customizable ditch, tile, and plane designs via Slope-IQ.
Is Ditch Assist suitable for different geographic locations and climatic conditions?While the manual doesn't explicitly mention about different geographic locations and climatic conditions, Ditch Assist is designed to be versatile and robust, potentially adaptable to different conditions. However, for specific conditions, it is recommended to consult the manufacturer or dealer.
How does Ditch Assist aid in water management on a farm?Ditch Assist aids in water management by providing fully customizable designs for surface drainage, as well as facilitating tile drainage installation.
Can Ditch Assist be integrated with new models of tractors?Yes, Ditch Assist can be integrated with any tractor, new or old, regardless of its make or model.
How does Ditch Assist support the import of land forming designs?Ditch Assist supports the import of land forming designs using advanced 3D designs in XYZ text format from service providers such as OptiSurface, AgForm3D, and Ezigrade.
How can Ditch Assist improve the efficiency of farming operations?Ditch Assist improves the efficiency of farming operations by providing a comprehensive, user-friendly, and cost-effective automatic machine control system that automates various tasks, optimizes water management, and facilitates seamless land forming.
What is the first step in setting up Ditch Assist?The first step in setting up Ditch Assist is to install the Ditch Assist hardware.
How should I connect the Power Harness?You should connect the Power Harness directly to the tractor battery terminals.
Where should I position the Ditch Assist Control Module?The Ditch Assist Control Module should be attached on the implement using the provided magnet mounts, ensuring a clear line-of-sight between the module and the cab.
How should I install the PWM Valve?The PWM valve should be installed on the implement or the rear of the tractor using the supplied bracket, or you may fabricate a custom mounting bracket if preferred.
Where should the RTK GNSS Antenna be positioned?The RTK GNSS Antenna should be attached on the implement at a location that moves up and down with the blade or cutting point, and should have an unobstructed view of the sky from all operating positions from 10 degrees above the horizon.
What settings should I set for the GNSS?You should set the GNSS to output GGA and VTG messages at 5Hz or 10Hz, with a baud rate of 38,400.
How do I connect the power cable in the Ditch Assist setup?You should attach the power cable to the main harness to connect it in the Ditch Assist setup.
How should I connect the GNSS to the Ditch Assist setup?You should use the GPS-specific cable for your device to connect GNSS to the main harness.
How should I connect the hydraulic raise and lower connectors in the Ditch Assist setup?You should attach the hydraulic raise and lower connectors to the solenoids on the PWM valve. The direction is not crucial at this point.
How can I protect the Ditch Assist Control Module?You can protect the Ditch Assist Control Module by using the provided plug connectors to seal the two empty ports on it. This step is important to prevent dirt and moisture from damaging the controller electronics.
How can I connect my tablet to the Ditch Assist Control Module?You can connect your tablet to the Ditch Assist Control Module by downloading and installing the Ditch Assist App onto your tablet, and then connecting your tablet to the DitchAssist wi-fi network using the password 12345678.
What should I do if my tablet alerts me about the network lacking an internet connection when connecting to the Ditch Assist Control Module?If your tablet alerts you about the network lacking an internet connection, you should select the option to continue with the connection.
How can I verify that my tablet is connected to the Ditch Assist Control Module?You can verify that your tablet is connected to the Ditch Assist Control Module by checking the Ditch Assist app to see if the Status details on the upper left corner of the screen state that you're connected to DitchAssistxxxx, with xxxx representing the unit's serial number.
What should the GNSS info panel display on the Ditch Assist App?The GNSS info panel on the Ditch Assist App should display details about your current positional coordinates, number of satellites, fix status, etc.
How can I verify implement hydraulic control from the Ditch Assist App?You can verify implement hydraulic control from the Ditch Assist App by accessing the Grading screen, pressing and maintaining the Implement Raise and Lower buttons on the tablet screen, and observing if the implement correspondingly rises and descends.
What should I do if pressing the raise button results in the implement lowering in the Ditch Assist App?If pressing the raise button results in the implement lowering in the Ditch Assist App, you should switch the cable connections to the valve solenoids. Then, retest to confirm the correct operation direction.
How can I start operating Ditch Assist?You can start operating Ditch Assist by running a survey and then using Slope-IQ to design a best-fit or customized drain profile, importing a 3D landforming design from your desktop software, or entering a straight grade or target elevation to perform simple grading or leveling.
How should I position the Ditch Assist Control Module?Attach the Ditch Assist Control Module on the implement using the provided magnet mounts, ensuring a clear line-of-sight between the module and the cab.
How do I set up the GNSS antenna for the Ditch Assist system?Attach the GNSS antenna on the implement at a location that moves up and down with the blade or cutting point. The antenna should have an unobstructed view of the sky from all operating positions from 10 degrees above the horizon.
What GNSS settings are recommended for the Ditch Assist system?Set the GNSS to output GGA and VTG messages at 5Hz or 10Hz, with a baud rate of 38,400.
How do I protect the Ditch Assist Control Module from damage?Use the provided plug connectors to seal the two empty ports on the Ditch Assist Control Module. This step is important to prevent dirt and moisture from damaging the controller electronics.
How do I confirm that I'm connected to the Ditch Assist Control Module via my tablet?Check the Ditch Assist app to confirm that you're connected to the Ditch Assist Control Module. This can be confirmed via the Status details on the upper left corner of the screen (it should state that you're connected to DitchAssistxxxx, with xxxx representing the unit's serial number).
How do I connect the hydraulic raise and lower connectors?Attach these to the solenoids on the PWM valve (direction is not crucial at this point).
How can I adjust the valve hydraulics to detent mode in Ditch Assist?You need to identify the hydraulic remotes that are connected to both the bypass and the valve - manipulating the bypass controls will result in the implement's elevation and depression. Then, adjust the valve hydraulics to detent mode, which allows for a steady flow.
How do I start operating the Ditch Assist system?To start operating the Ditch Assist system, run a survey and then use Slope-IQ to design a best-fit or customized drain profile, or import a 3D landforming design from your desktop software, or enter a straight grade or target elevation to perform simple grading or leveling.
How should I connect the power cable for Ditch Assist?Connect the power cable to the main harness as part of the Ditch Assist setup.
How can I download and install the Ditch Assist App onto my tablet?You can download the Ditch Assist App from the app store of your tablet, then follow the on-screen instructions to install it.
How do I ensure that the Ditch Assist Control Module is switched ON?Check if the LED lights are glowing on the module. If they are, it indicates that the Control Module is switched ON.
What should I do if the GNSS info panel remains empty on the Ditch Assist app?If the GNSS info panel remains empty on the Ditch Assist app, refer to the GNSS settings and troubleshooting parts of the user manual.
What should I do if I have a problem with the hydraulic control from the Ditch Assist app?If pressing the raise button results in the implement lowering, you should switch the cable connections to the valve solenoids and retest to confirm the correct operation direction.
How do I connect my tablet to the Ditch Assist wi-fi network?Go to your tablet's wi-fi settings, search for a wi-fi network name that starts with DitchAssist, and connect to it using the password 12345678.
How can I check if I'm connected to the Ditch Assist Control Module?Check the Ditch Assist app. The Status details on the upper left corner of the screen should state that you're connected to DitchAssistxxxx, with xxxx representing the unit's serial number.
How can I use the Ditch Assist App to control the implement's elevation?You can use the Implement Raise and Lower buttons on the Grading screen of the Ditch Assist App to control the implement's elevation.
How can I customize the operation of the Ditch Assist system?You can customize the operation of the Ditch Assist system by running a survey and using Slope-IQ to design a best-fit or customized drain profile, or by importing a 3D landforming design from your desktop software.
How do I connect the GNSS to the main harness in the Ditch Assist setup?Use the GPS-specific cable for your device to connect the GNSS to the main harness.
How can I protect the Ditch Assist Control Module from dirt and moisture?To protect the Ditch Assist Control Module from dirt and moisture, use the provided plug connectors to seal the two empty ports on the module.
What is the password for connecting to the Ditch Assist wi-fi network?The password for connecting to the Ditch Assist wi-fi network is 12345678.
What should I do if my tablet alerts me that the Ditch Assist network lacks an internet connection?If your tablet alerts you that the Ditch Assist network lacks an internet connection, you should select the option to continue with the connection.
How do I confirm that the Ditch Assist system is receiving my current positional coordinates?You can confirm this by checking the GNSS info panel on the Ditch Assist app. It should display details about your current positional coordinates, number of satellites, fix status, etc.
How can I operate the Ditch Assist system for simple grading or leveling?You can operate the Ditch Assist system for simple grading or leveling by entering a straight grade or target elevation in the system.
How do I connect the Hydraulic Raise and Lower Connectors in the Ditch Assist setup?To connect the Hydraulic Raise and Lower Connectors in the Ditch Assist setup, attach these to the solenoids on the PWM valve. The direction of the connection is not crucial at this point.
How do I start using the Ditch Assist system after installation and setup?To start using the Ditch Assist system after installation and setup, run a survey and then use Slope-IQ to design a best-fit or customized drain profile, or import a 3D landforming design from your desktop software, or enter a straight grade or target elevation to perform simple grading or leveling.
How do I connect the power cable in the Ditch Assist system?To connect the power cable in the Ditch Assist system, you need to attach the power cable to the main harness.
What should I do if the implement lowers when I press the raise button in the Ditch Assist App?If the implement lowers when you press the raise button in the Ditch Assist App, you should switch the cable connections to the valve solenoids. Then, retest to confirm the correct operation direction.
How do I confirm the correct operation direction of the implement in the Ditch Assist App?To confirm the correct operation direction of the implement in the Ditch Assist App, press and maintain the Implement Raise and Lower buttons on the tablet screen. The implement should correspondingly rise and descend.
How do I check the Status details in the Ditch Assist App?The Status details can be found on the upper left corner of the Ditch Assist App screen.
What does the Ditch Assist Control Module LED lights indicate?The LED lights on the Ditch Assist Control Module indicate whether the module is switched ON or not. If the lights are glowing, it means the module is switched ON.
How do I connect my tablet to the Ditch Assist Control Module?To connect your tablet to the Ditch Assist Control Module, you should first download and install the Ditch Assist App onto your tablet. Then go to your tablet's wi-fi settings, search for a wi-fi network name that starts with DitchAssist, and connect using the password 12345678.
What does the GNSS info panel on the Ditch Assist App display?The GNSS info panel on the Ditch Assist App displays details about your current positional coordinates, number of satellites, fix status, etc.
How can I control the implement's elevation using the Ditch Assist App?You can control the implement's elevation using the Ditch Assist App by pressing and maintaining the Implement Raise and Lower buttons on the Grading screen of the app.
How can I perform simple grading or leveling using the Ditch Assist system?You can perform simple grading or leveling using the Ditch Assist system by entering a straight grade or target elevation in the system.
What should I do if my tablet can't connect to the Ditch Assist Control Module?If your tablet can't connect to the Ditch Assist Control Module, ensure that the Control Module is switched ON, and that you are entering the correct wi-fi password (12345678). If problems persist, refer to the troubleshooting parts of the user manual.
How can I run a survey using the Ditch Assist system?Detailed instructions for running a survey using the Ditch Assist system can be found in the appropriate chapters of the user manual. In brief, after setup, you can run a survey and use Slope-IQ to design a best-fit or customized drain profile.
How can I import a 3D landforming design using the Ditch Assist system?Detailed instructions for importing a 3D landforming design using the Ditch Assist system can be found in the appropriate chapters of the user manual. In brief, after setup, you can import a 3D landforming design from your desktop software.
How do I connect the Ditch Assist Control Module to the main harness?To connect the Ditch Assist Control Module to the main harness, attach the main harness to the Ditch Assist Control Module. The connectors will only fit into their corresponding ports on the controller.
How do I identify the hydraulic remotes connected to the bypass and the valve in the Ditch Assist system?The specifics may vary depending on your particular tractor model and hydraulic system. Typically, this involves testing the different hydraulic remotes to see which ones result in the implement's elevation and depression when manipulated. Detailed information should be available in your tractor's user manual or hydraulic system documentation.
How can I design a best-fit or customized drain profile using the Ditch Assist system?To design a best-fit or customized drain profile using the Ditch Assist system, you should first run a survey. Then use the Slope-IQ feature in the system to create the profile.
How can I set up the PWM valve in the Ditch Assist system?Install the PWM valve on the implement or the rear of the tractor using the supplied bracket, or you may fabricate a custom mounting bracket if preferred.
How do I prevent dirt and moisture from damaging the Ditch Assist Control Module?To prevent dirt and moisture from damaging the Ditch Assist Control Module, use the provided plug connectors to seal the two empty ports on the module.
How do I connect the power harness in the Ditch Assist system?Connect the Power Harness directly to the tractor battery terminals. Refrain from connecting it to a trailer power connector or an in-cab power strip.
How do I verify the hydraulic control of the implement from the Ditch Assist App?To verify the hydraulic control of the implement from the Ditch Assist App, press and maintain the Implement Raise and Lower buttons on the Grading screen of the app. The implement should correspondingly rise and descend.
How do I connect the GNSS to the main harness in the Ditch Assist system?To connect the GNSS to the main harness in the Ditch Assist system, use the GPS-specific cable for your device.
How do I connect the Hydraulic Raise and Lower Connectors to the PWM valve in the Ditch Assist system?To connect the Hydraulic Raise and Lower Connectors to the PWM valve in the Ditch Assist system, attach these to the solenoids on the PWM valve. The direction of the connection is not crucial at this point.
How do I know if I am correctly connected to the Ditch Assist Control Module via my tablet?Check the Ditch Assist app to confirm that you're connected to the Ditch Assist Control Module. This can be confirmed via the Status details on the upper left corner of the screen. It should state that you're connected to DitchAssistxxxx, with xxxx representing the unit's serial number.
How do I use the Grading screen in the Ditch Assist App?The Grading screen in the Ditch Assist App can be accessed by tapping on the Grading tab button. From here, you can control the implement's elevation by pressing and maintaining the Implement Raise and Lower buttons.
What is the recommended GNSS setting for the Ditch Assist system?For the Ditch Assist system, it's recommended to set the GNSS to output GGA and VTG messages at 5Hz or 10Hz, with a baud rate of 38,400.
How do I adjust the valve hydraulics to detent mode in the Ditch Assist system?In the Ditch Assist system, adjust the valve hydraulics to detent mode, which allows for a steady flow. You will need to identify the hydraulic remotes that are connected to both the bypass and the valve first.
How do I check the GNSS info panel on the Ditch Assist App?You can check the GNSS info panel on the Ditch Assist App by looking for details about your current positional coordinates, number of satellites, fix status, etc. If the panel remains empty, refer to the GNSS settings and troubleshooting parts of the user manual.
How can I download the Ditch Assist App?You can download the Ditch Assist App from the app store of your tablet. After downloading, follow the on-screen instructions to install it.
How do I connect the main harness to the Ditch Assist Control Module?To connect the main harness to the Ditch Assist Control Module, you need to attach the main harness to the module. The connectors will only fit into their corresponding ports on the controller.
How do I position the PWM valve in the Ditch Assist system?To position the PWM valve in the Ditch Assist system, install the valve on the implement or the rear of the tractor using the supplied bracket, or you may fabricate a custom mounting bracket if preferred.
How do I position the Ditch Assist Control Module?To position the Ditch Assist Control Module, attach the module on the implement using the provided magnet mounts, ensuring a clear line-of-sight between the module and the cab.
What should I do if the GNSS info panel remains empty in the Ditch Assist App?If the GNSS info panel remains empty in the Ditch Assist App, you should refer to the GNSS settings and troubleshooting parts of the user manual.
How can I control the implement's elevation and depression in the Ditch Assist system?In the Ditch Assist system, you can control the implement's elevation and depression by manipulating the hydraulic remotes that are connected to both the bypass and the valve.
How do I install the Ditch Assist hardware?To install the Ditch Assist hardware, you need to connect the Power Harness directly to the tractor battery terminals, position the Ditch Assist Control Module and the PWM valve, set up the RTK GNSS Antenna, configure the GNSS settings, and use the Ditch Assist harnesses to connect all parts.
How can I find the serial number of my Ditch Assist unit?You can find the serial number of your Ditch Assist unit in the Status details on the upper left corner of the Ditch Assist App screen. It's represented as DitchAssistxxxx, with xxxx being the serial number.
How can I use the Ditch Assist App to control the implement's rise and descent?You can use the Implement Raise and Lower buttons on the Grading screen of the Ditch Assist App to control the implement's rise and descent.
How can I switch the cable connections to the valve solenoids in the Ditch Assist system?You can switch the cable connections to the valve solenoids in the Ditch Assist system by simply disconnecting the existing connections and reconnecting them in the opposite configuration. If pressing the raise button on the Ditch Assist App results in the implement lowering, this would be the recommended action.
How can I protect the Ditch Assist Control Module from damage?You can protect the Ditch Assist Control Module from damage by using the provided plug connectors to seal the two empty ports on the module. This step is important to prevent dirt and moisture from damaging the controller electronics.
How can I operate the Ditch Assist system for custom landforming?You can operate the Ditch Assist system for custom landforming by importing a 3D landforming design from your desktop software.
How do I attach the GNSS antenna in the Ditch Assist system?Attach the GNSS antenna on the implement at a location that moves up and down with the blade or cutting point. The antenna should have an unobstructed view of the sky from all operating positions from 10 degrees above the horizon.
How do I set up the RTK GNSS Antenna in the Ditch Assist system?To set up the RTK GNSS Antenna in the Ditch Assist system, attach the antenna on the implement at a location that moves up and down with the blade or cutting point. The antenna should have an unobstructed view of the sky from all operating positions from 10 degrees above the horizon.
How do I ensure a clear line-of-sight between the Ditch Assist Control Module and the cab?To ensure a clear line-of-sight between the Ditch Assist Control Module and the cab, position the module on the implement using the provided magnet mounts. Ensure that there are no obstructions between the module and the cab.
How can I adjust the implement's elevation in the Ditch Assist App?You can adjust the implement's elevation in the Ditch Assist App by pressing and maintaining the Implement Raise and Lower buttons on the Grading screen. If the implement moves in the opposite direction, switch the cable connections to the valve solenoids.
How do I connect the power cable in the Ditch Assist system?To connect the power cable in the Ditch Assist system, attach the power cable to the main harness.
How can I connect the GNSS to the Ditch Assist system?To connect the GNSS to the Ditch Assist system, set the GNSS to output GGA and VTG messages at 5Hz or 10Hz, with a baud rate of 38,400. Then, use the GPS-specific cable for your device to connect GNSS to the main harness.
How can I adjust the valve hydraulics to detent mode in the Ditch Assist system?In the Ditch Assist system, adjust the valve hydraulics to detent mode, which allows for a steady flow. This is done by manipulating the hydraulic remotes that are connected to both the bypass and the valve.
How can I check if I'm correctly connected to the Ditch Assist Control Module via my tablet?You can check if you're correctly connected to the Ditch Assist Control Module via your tablet by confirming the Status details on the upper left corner of the Ditch Assist App screen. It should state that you're connected to DitchAssistxxxx, with xxxx representing the unit's serial number.
How can I use the Ditch Assist App to verify implement hydraulic control?You can use the Ditch Assist App to verify implement hydraulic control by pressing and maintaining the Implement Raise and Lower buttons on the Grading screen. The implement should correspondingly rise and descend. If the opposite happens, switch the cable connections to the valve solenoids and retest.
What is the password for the DitchAssist wi-fi network?The password for the DitchAssist wi-fi network is 12345678.
How do I connect to the DitchAssist wi-fi network on my tablet?To connect to the DitchAssist wi-fi network on your tablet, go to your tablet's wi-fi settings, search for a wi-fi network name that starts with DitchAssist, and connect using the password 12345678.
What information should I check to ensure that my tablet is connected to the Ditch Assist Control Module?You should check the Status details on the upper left corner of the Ditch Assist App screen to ensure that your tablet is connected to the Ditch Assist Control Module. It should state that you're connected to DitchAssistxxxx, with xxxx representing the unit's serial number.
How do I connect the Hydraulic Raise and Lower Connectors in the Ditch Assist system?To connect the Hydraulic Raise and Lower Connectors in the Ditch Assist system, attach these connectors to the solenoids on the PWM valve. The direction is not crucial at this point.
What should I do if pressing the raise button in the Ditch Assist App results in the implement lowering?If pressing the raise button in the Ditch Assist App results in the implement lowering, switch the cable connections to the valve solenoids, then retest to confirm the correct operation direction.
How do I set the GNSS settings in the Ditch Assist system?To set the GNSS settings in the Ditch Assist system, configure the GNSS to output GGA and VTG messages at 5Hz or 10Hz, with a baud rate of 38,400.
How do I begin operating the Ditch Assist system?You can begin operating the Ditch Assist system by running a survey and then using Slope-IQ to design a best-fit or customized drain profile, importing a 3D landforming design from your desktop software, or entering a straight grade or target elevation to perform simple grading or leveling.
What are the recommended GNSS settings for the Ditch Assist system?For the Ditch Assist system, it's recommended to set the GNSS to output GGA and VTG messages at 5Hz or 10Hz, with a baud rate of 38,400.
How can I perform simple grading or leveling using the Ditch Assist system?You can perform simple grading or leveling using the Ditch Assist system by entering a straight grade or target elevation in the system.
How do I know if the GNSS is functioning properly in the Ditch Assist system?You can check if the GNSS is functioning properly in the Ditch Assist system by looking at the GNSS info panel on the Ditch Assist App. It should display details about your current positional coordinates, number of satellites, fix status, etc. If the panel remains empty, refer to the GNSS settings and troubleshooting parts of the user manual.
How can I design a drain profile using the Ditch Assist system?You can design a drain profile using the Ditch Assist system by running a survey and then using Slope-IQ to design a best-fit or customized drain profile.
How do I connect my tablet to the Ditch Assist Control Module?To connect your tablet to the Ditch Assist Control Module, ensure that the module is switched on. Then, download and install the Ditch Assist App onto your tablet, connect to the DitchAssist wi-fi network, and check the app to confirm connection.
How do I ensure that the Ditch Assist Control Module is switched on?To ensure that the Ditch Assist Control Module is switched on, check if the LED lights are glowing on the module.
What should I do if my tablet alerts me about the DitchAssist network lacking an internet connection?If your tablet alerts you about the DitchAssist network lacking an internet connection, select the option to continue with the connection.
What should I do if the Ditch Assist App does not confirm that I am connected to the Ditch Assist Control Module?If the Ditch Assist App does not confirm that you are connected to the Ditch Assist Control Module, check your tablet's wi-fi settings to ensure you are connected to the DitchAssist network, and try restarting the app. If the problem persists, refer to the troubleshooting section of the user manual.
How do I use the Ditch Assist App to control the implement's elevation?You can use the Ditch Assist App to control the implement's elevation by pressing and maintaining the Implement Raise and Lower buttons on the Grading screen of the app. If pressing the raise button results in the implement lowering, you may need to switch the cable connections to the valve solenoids.
How can I configure the GNSS settings in the Ditch Assist system?To configure the GNSS settings in the Ditch Assist system, set the GNSS to output GGA and VTG messages at 5Hz or 10Hz, with a baud rate of 38,400.
How do I connect the main harness to the Ditch Assist Control Module?To connect the main harness to the Ditch Assist Control Module, simply attach the main harness to the Ditch Assist Control Module. The connectors will only fit into their corresponding ports on the controller.
How can I design a best-fit or customized drain profile using the Ditch Assist system?You can design a best-fit or customized drain profile using the Ditch Assist system by running a survey and then using Slope-IQ.
How do I attach the power cable to the main harness in the Ditch Assist system?To attach the power cable to the main harness in the Ditch Assist system, use the Ditch Assist harnesses and connect the power cable to the main harness.
What are the steps to install the Ditch Assist hardware?The steps to install the Ditch Assist hardware include connecting the Power Harness to the tractor battery terminals, positioning the Ditch Assist Control Module and the PWM valve, setting up the RTK GNSS Antenna, configuring the GNSS settings, and connecting all the components using the Ditch Assist harnesses.
How can I ensure the best connection between the Ditch Assist Control Module and my tablet?To ensure the best connection between the Ditch Assist Control Module and your tablet, make sure that the Control Module is switched on, that your tablet is connected to the DitchAssist wi-fi network, and that the Ditch Assist App confirms your connection to the Control Module.
How do I connect the GNSS to the main harness in the Ditch Assist system?To connect the GNSS to the main harness in the Ditch Assist system, use the GPS-specific cable for your device.
What steps should I take to start using the Ditch Assist system?To start using the Ditch Assist system, install the hardware, connect the tablet to the Ditch Assist Control Module, verify implement hydraulic control from the Ditch Assist App, and then start operating the system according to the specific tasks you want to perform.
How do I connect the Hydraulic Raise and Lower Connectors to the solenoids on the PWM valve?To connect the Hydraulic Raise and Lower Connectors to the solenoids on the PWM valve, simply attach these connectors to the solenoids. The direction is not crucial at this point.
How do I connect the power cable in the Ditch Assist system?To connect the power cable in the Ditch Assist system, attach the power cable to the main harness.
What should I do if the implement lowers when I press the raise button in the Ditch Assist App?If the implement lowers when you press the raise button in the Ditch Assist App, switch the cable connections to the valve solenoids. Then, retest to confirm the correct operation direction.
How do I use the Ditch Assist App to control the implement's descent?You can use the Ditch Assist App to control the implement's descent by pressing and maintaining the Implement Raise and Lower buttons on the Grading screen of the app. If pressing the lower button results in the implement raising, you may need to switch the cable connections to the valve solenoids.
How do I connect my tablet to the Ditch Assist Control Module?To connect your tablet to the Ditch Assist Control Module, download and install the Ditch Assist App onto your tablet. Then, connect to the DitchAssist wi-fi network using the provided password, and check the app to confirm connection.
How do I protect the Ditch Assist Control Module from dirt and moisture?To protect the Ditch Assist Control Module from dirt and moisture, use the provided plug connectors to seal the two empty ports on the module.
How can I perform simple grading or leveling using the Ditch Assist system?To perform simple grading or leveling using the Ditch Assist system, enter a straight grade or target elevation in the system.
How do I connect the Hydraulic Raise and Lower Connectors in the Ditch Assist system?To connect the Hydraulic Raise and Lower Connectors in the Ditch Assist system, attach these connectors to the solenoids on the PWM valve. The direction is not crucial at this point.
How do I check if I'm correctly connected to the Ditch Assist Control Module via my tablet?You can check if you're correctly connected to the Ditch Assist Control Module via your tablet by confirming the Status details on the upper left corner of the Ditch Assist App screen. It should state that you're connected to DitchAssistxxxx, with xxxx representing the unit's serial number.
What is the recommended position for the PWM valve in the Ditch Assist system?The recommended position for the PWM valve in the Ditch Assist system is on the implement or the rear of the tractor. You can use the supplied bracket for this, or fabricate a custom mounting bracket if preferred.
How can I import a 3D landforming design from my desktop software to the Ditch Assist system?To import a 3D landforming design from your desktop software to the Ditch Assist system, use the import function in the Ditch Assist system. This process may vary depending on the specific desktop software you're using.
How can I configure the GPS settings in the Ditch Assist system?Set the GPS to output GGA and VTG messages at 5Hz or 10Hz, with a baud rate of 38,400.
How do I set up the GPS antenna in the Ditch Assist system?Attach the GPS antenna on the implement at a location that moves up and down with the blade or cutting point. The antenna should have an unobstructed view of the sky from all operating positions from 10 degrees above the horizon.
How do I connect the GPS to the main harness in the Ditch Assist system?To connect the GPS to the main harness in the Ditch Assist system, use the GPS-specific cable for your device.
How can I ensure the GPS info panel is filled in the Ditch Assist system?The GPS info panel should display details about your current positional coordinates, number of satellites, fix status, etc. If this panel remains empty, refer to the GPS settings and troubleshooting parts of this user manual.
What are the correct GPS settings for the Ditch Assist system?For the Ditch Assist system, set the GPS to output GGA and VTG messages at 5Hz or 10Hz, with a baud rate of 38,400.
How can I check that the GPS is functioning correctly in the Ditch Assist system?You can check that the GPS is functioning correctly in the Ditch Assist system by ensuring that the GPS info panel in the Ditch Assist app is filled - it should display details about your current positional coordinates, number of satellites, fix status, etc. If this panel remains empty, refer to the GPS settings and troubleshooting parts of this user manual.
